title: Bogga
content:

{{Quote|Make way for Bogga the Hutt.|Gudb|audio=BoggatheHutt2-TOTJ.flac|<ref name="audio">[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]]</ref>}}
'''Bogga''' was a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] who operated out of the [[Stenness Node]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] leading up to the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Bogga was the head of a [[Bogga's criminal empire|criminal empire]] that encompassed the Stenness [[Star system/Legends|system]], and led an [[Bogga's enforcer gang|enforcer gang]] that was feared throughout the area. In [[3999 BBY]], Bogga received word that a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] possessing valuable [[Adegan crystal/Legends|Adegan crystals]] had landed on the [[Stenness Hyperspace Terminal]], where the Hutt often dwelt. Desiring the jewels, Bogga sent several of his enforcers to obtain them. The Jedi Knight, [[Andur Sunrider]], was [[Death/Legends|slain]] in the [[Confrontation on the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal|ensuing struggle]], forcing his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]], [[Nomi Sunrider|Nomi]], to take up his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and strike down two of Bogga's henchmen, while depriving the third of the [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]]. Bent on obtaining the gems, Bogga followed the [[Gender/Legends|woman]] in his [[Sail barge/Legends|sail barge]] to the desolate [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]], where she was to deliver the jewels to the Hutt's sworn enemy, a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] named [[Thon]]. Bogga was again foiled when Thon and his [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]], [[Oss Wilum]], defeated the Hutt and his crew.

Bogga subsequently returned to his [[Bogga's palace|stronghold]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Vo Dasha]], although he remained committed to exacting revenge on Thon and obtaining the Adegan crystals. In the meantime, Bogga loaned a security crew to protect an [[Ithullan ore hauler|Ithullian ore hauler]], a ship that was under the threat of attack by [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]]. The pirates were defeated, and their leader, a being named [[Finhead Stonebone]], was captured and brought to Vo Dasha in [[Prisoner/Legends|chains]]. Bogga decided to spare Stonebone's life on one condition: that Stonebone would journey to Ambria, kill Thon, and obtain the crystals. Stonebone consented to the arrangement and left for Ambria. However, this third attempt to obtain the crystals failed like the previous two, as Thon and the newly [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Nomi Sunrider defeated Stonebone in a [[Skirmish on Ambria|pitched battle]].

==Biography==
===Search for the Adegan crystals===
====Stenness Hyperspace Terminal====
{{Quote|Get them Gudb, and Bogga the Hutt will reward you.|Bogga|audio=BoggtheHutt-TOTJ.flac|<ref name="audio" />}}
[[File:Gudb.jpg|thumb|left|Bogga orders his enforcers to steal the crystals.]]
Bogga the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]] and lived in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] leading up to the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. He grew to become a powerful and feared [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] who operated mainly out of the [[Stenness Node|Stenness system]] in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] Territories. Bogga also assembled a [[Bogga's enforcer gang|group]] of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], thugs, and [[Gangster/Legends|gangsters]] that terrorized the area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] under his control. At its height, the Hutt's [[Bogga's criminal empire|criminal empire]] branched out six [[Parsec/Legends|parsecs]] in every direction. Bogga maintained a [[Bogga's palace|private residence]] on the Stenness [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Vo Dasha]] and frequented the [[Stenness Hyperspace Terminal]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]].<ref name="companion" />

At some point in [[Time/Legends|time]], Bogga made a trip to the desolate Stenness Node planet [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]], where he found a [[hssiss]] dragon in a [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]–infused [[Lake Natth|lake]]. He named the beast [[Ktriss]] and made it his pet and familiar, often using her to threaten his enemies or underlings that had failed him.<ref name="audio" /> In [[3999 BBY]],<ref name="SWI28 date">{{InsiderCite|26|The Horse's Mouth|Straight from the Horse's Mouth: A Guide to the ''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'' Universe, Part 1}} dates the events of [[Tales of the Jedi 3|''Tales of the Jedi'' 3]] to [[3999 BBY]].</ref> several members of Bogga's gang informed him that a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] that was on its way to Ambria with a shipment of valuable [[Adegan crystal/Legends|Adegan crystals]] had stopped on the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal. Bogga had a client that would [[Currency/Legends|pay]] a grand sum for the [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]]<ref name="nomi1" /> and felt that he could not afford to miss this opportunity, as an influx of capital could potentially enable him to overtake several [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] closer to the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="companion" /> He ordered three of his underlings, [[Gudb]], [[Quanto]], and [[Rek (enforcer)|Rek]], to retrieve the cargo. However, the Hutt advised caution, as the ship, the ''[[Lightside Explorer]]'', was owned and operated by a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], [[Andur Sunrider]].<ref name="nomi1" />

Bogga's thugs [[Confrontation on the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal|accosted]] Sunrider on the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal [[Hangar/Legends|docking bay]], where the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] was accompanied by his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]], [[Nomi Sunrider|Nomi]]; his baby [[Sunrider family|daughter]], [[Vima Sunrider|Vima]]; and his [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], [[A-3DO]]. While Rek and Quanto distracted Sunrider, Gudb released his pet [[Gorm-worm/Legends|gorm worm]], [[Skritch]], who [[Death/Legends|fatally bit]] the Jedi on the neck. Taking up her fallen [[Gender/Legends|husband's]] [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], the grieving Nomi slew Quanto and then Rek, forcing Gudb to flee back to Bogga without the crystals. Bogga brought the rest of his enforcers and followed the ''Lightside Explorer'' in his [[Sail barge/Legends|sail barge]], the ''[[Twilight Sailer]]''.<ref name="nomi1" />

====Ambria====
{{Quote|Finish that Jedi, Gudb! Soon I'll have those pretty crystals!|Bogga, on Oss Wilum, to Gudb|audio=BoggaFinishThatJedi-TotJAudio.flac|<ref name="audio" />}}
[[File:Thon_HS.png|thumb|right|250px|Bogga's crew was attacked by Jedi Master Thon (pictured) on Ambria]]
The chase brought Bogga and his crew to Ambria, where Sunrider had gone to find [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Thon]]. While in the skies above Ambria, and after threatening to let Ktriss kill and [[Diet/Legends|devour]] Gudb, Bogga gave Gudb another chance and sent him with a team of enforcers to the surface of Ambria to retrieve the crystals while he remained in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]]. The enforcers boarded the ''Lightside Explorer'', only to find that the Adegan crystals were not on board. However, Gudb discovered that the prize was at [[Ambria Jedi Enclave|Master Thon's compound]], along with Nomi Sunrider.<ref name="nomi1" />

Gudb relayed this news to Bogga, who ordered his [[Convoy/Legends|convoy]] to travel to Thon's compound. Upon reaching Thon's home, Bogga spied a herd of [[Staga/Legends|staga]] beasts and told his underlings to slaughter the animals for [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]]. While Bogga and his lackeys worked, they were confronted by a single Jedi, the [[Vultan]] [[Oss Wilum]], Thon's [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]]. Outnumbered, Wilum was quickly defeated, and Bogga ordered Gudb to execute the Jedi. Suddenly, Gudb was interrupted by a large beast—revealed to be Thon himself—that attacked and decimated Bogga's group. Realizing that they had no chance of survival if they stayed and fought, Bogga's crew fled the encounter.<ref name="nomi1" />

===Dealings with Stonebone===
{{Quote|I have but ''one'' enemy I cannot forgive. A lowly ''beast'' who dares to name himself 'Master Thon.' This so-called ''Jedi'' has something ''precious'' that rightfully belongs to Bogga! ''Kill'' this 'Master Thon' for me, and I will overlook your despicable crimes. Bring me the Adegan crystals and I might even let you ''work'' for me.|Bogga, to Finhead Stonebone|<ref name="nomi2">[[Tales of the Jedi 4|''Tales of the Jedi'' 4]]</ref>}}
[[File:KtrissBogga-TotJ4.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Bogga with Ktriss on Vo Dasha]]
Some time after the failed raid on Ambria, Bogga was paid a fortune by [[Gruna]], the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of an [[Ithullan ore hauler|Ithullian ore hauler]], to protect his ship from pirates. Bogga sent him a security detail that was put to work when the ship was attacked by [[Ore/Legends|ore]] raiders. However, Bogga's henchmen were quickly and easily killed by the pirates, who were led by an individual named [[Finhead Stonebone]]. Although the pirates believed that they had captured the vessel, Bogga's enforcers surprised them by dropping out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] in the Hutt's [[Dreadnaught/Legends|dreadnaught]], [[Enforcer One (dreadnaught)|''Enforcer One'']]. The group captured Stonebone and his ship, the ''[[Marauder Starjacker]]'', transporting them to Bogga's private moon, Vo Dasha. At the time, Bogga was sitting on his [[Throne/Legends|throne]] alongside Ktriss, berating Gudb for failing to obtain the Adegan crystals. Receiving word that the pirates had been brought to Vo Dasha, Bogga ordered his servants to bring Stonebone and the other pirates before him. Bogga threatened Stonebone at first, then told him that his crimes would be overlooked if he killed Thon and retrieved the crystals. However, Stonebone refused. An angered Bogga decided to release Ktriss on the [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]], and the beast devoured Stonebone's [[First Mate/Legends|First Mate]], [[Roko]].<ref name="nomi2" />

As Ktriss ate the remains of Roko, Stonebone decided to accept Bogga's offer. Pleased by Stonebone's submission, the Hutt ordered the pirate to carry out his mission at once.<ref name="nomi3">[[Tales of the Jedi 5|''Tales of the Jedi'' 5]]</ref> Bogga also ordered Gudb to accompany them and gave him [[Mandalorian manacles|chains]] capable of subduing even a Jedi.<ref name="audio" /> Finhead Stonebone took a [[Finhead Stonebone's pirate gang|group]] of his pirate associates and several of Bogga's enforcers along with him to Ambria, where they broke into Thon's compound and retrieved the Adegan crystals. On their way back to their ship, however, the group was interrupted by the arrival of Thon and Jedi [[Jedi training/Legends|trainee]] Nomi Sunrider. Stonebone and the pirates were promptly [[Skirmish on Ambria|attacked]] and chased off by the two Jedi, and Stonebone limped back to Bogga without the Adegan crystals, with Thon suspecting that Bogga would humiliate the pirates even more for their failure.<ref name="nomi3" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|But Great Bogga is also called <nowiki>'</nowiki>''The Merciful One'',' and with good reason.|Bogga|<ref name="nomi2" />}}
[[File:Hutt04.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Bogga the Hutt]]
Bogga was a ruthless, unscrupulous Hutt who chose to make his living through crime. He commanded the respect of his enforcers and was widely feared throughout the Stenness Node, but would quickly berate and threaten his underlings if they failed him in any way. Bogga held respect for the Jedi Knights' [[Force power/Legends|abilities]], although he did not hesitate in ordering Gudb to kill Andur Sunrider.<ref name="nomi1" /> The Hutt often started sentences in his native [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]] [[Language/Legends|language]].<ref name="nomi2" /> Additionally, Bogga often referred to himself in the third person.<ref name="nomi3" /> He developed an obsession with Andur Sunrider's Adegan crystals and sanctioned repeated missions to retrieve them at any cost.<ref name="nomi2" /> He also developed a deep and unrelenting hatred toward Master Thon, referring to him a "lowly beast" and ordering Finhead Stonebone to kill him.<ref name="nomi3" /> Bogga fawned over his pet, Ktriss, and it could almost always be found by his side. The crime lord often threatened to unleash Ktriss on both enemies and underlings who had failed him. Bogga preferred to be addressed with ornate and elaborate titles, such as "Great and Well-Proportioned Bogga" or just "Great Bogga." He also called himself "The Merciful One" and saw a compassionate side to himself. Because of this, Bogga decided to spare Finhead Stonebone, despite his hatred of Stonebone's pirate gang.<ref name="nomi2" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|''Ne mafuta''! Land the sail barge! I want my Adegan crystals!|Bogga|<ref name="nomi1" />}}
Bogga [[Smoking/Legends|smoked]] a [[Hookah pipe/Legends|hookah pipe]]. Bogga owned the sail barge ''Twilight Sailer''<ref name="nomi1" /> and the dreadnought ''Enforcer One''.<ref name="nomi2" />

==Behind the scenes==
Bogga first appeared in the [[Tales of the Jedi 3|third issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, which was written by [[Tom Veitch]], illustrated by [[Janine Johnston]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="nomi1" /> on [[December 21]], [[1993]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54568/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_1993_3|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (1993) #3}}</ref> Bogga was voiced by [[Bud Thorpe]] in<ref name="audio" /> the [[1995]]<ref name="Amazon">{{Amazon|url=Star-Wars-Tales-Tom-Veitch/dp/1570421382|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi}}</ref> [[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]], based on the comics written by Veitch and adapted by [[John Whitman]].<ref name="audio" />
